title: DataQuality
content:
# DataQuality

Inherits from: ApiServiceModel


## Methods


### delete_bulk_data_quality(data: Optional[Union[requests.BulkDataQualityDelete, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> None

Deletes multiple data quality configurations.


### upsert_bulk_data_quality(data: Optional[Union[requests.BulkDataQualityUpsert, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> None

Create or update multiple data quality configurations at once.


### create_data_quality(data: Optional[Union[requests.DataQualityCreate, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.DataQualityCreate

Create a new Data Quality Configuration.


### list_data_quality(pagination_type: Optional[Literal[limits, cursor, stream]], page_size: Optional[int], page: Optional[int], next: Optional[str], previous: Optional[str], direction: Optional[Literal[asc, desc]], search: Optional[Sequence[str]], sort_by: Optional[Sequence[str]], data: Optional[Union[requests.DataQualityList, Mapping[str, object]]], fetch: bool, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> Union[KList[type.DataQuality], responses.DataQualityListPaginatedResponseCursor]

List data quality configurations based on the provided filters.


### delete_data_quality(resource: str, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> None

Delete a Data Quality configuration by resource.


### get_data_quality(resource: str, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.DataQualityGet

Get a Data Quality configuration by resource.


### update_data_quality(resource: str, data: Optional[Union[requests.DataQualityUpdate, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.DataQualityUpdate

Update a Data Quality by providing the new configuration. This request changes the whole Data Quality Configuration.


### bulk_delete_data_quality_metrics(data: Optional[Union[requests.DeleteDataQualityMetricsBulk, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> None

Delete multiple Data Quality Metrics by their names in a single request.


### bulk_upsert_data_quality_metrics(data: Optional[Union[requests.UpsertDataQualityMetricsBulk, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> None

Create or update multiple Data Quality Metrics in a single request.


### create_data_quality_metric(data: Optional[Union[requests.DataQualityMetricCreate, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.DataQualityMetricCreate

Create a new Data Quality Metric.


### list_data_quality_metrics(pagination_type: Optional[Literal[limits, cursor, stream]], page_size: Optional[int], page: Optional[int], next: Optional[str], previous: Optional[str], direction: Optional[Literal[asc, desc]], search: Optional[Sequence[str]], sort_by: Optional[Sequence[str]], data: Optional[Union[requests.DataQualityMetricsList, Mapping[str, object]]], fetch: bool, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> Union[KList[type.DataQualityMetric], responses.DataQualityMetricsListPaginatedResponseCursor]

Returns a list of Data Quality Metrics and its parameters. The Data Quality Metrics can be optionally filtered and sorted on the server before being returned.


### delete_data_quality_metric(metric_name: str, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> None

Delete a Data Quality Metric by name.


### get_data_quality_metric(metric_name: str, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.DataQualityMetricGet

Retrieve the parameters of a Data Quality Metric.


### update_data_quality_metric(metric_name: str, data: Optional[Union[requests.DataQualityMetricUpdate, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.DataQualityMetricUpdate

Update an existing Data Quality Metric.


### data_quality_simulate(data: Optional[Union[requests.DataQualitySimulate, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.DataQualitySimulate

Simulate the effect of data quality updates without making permanent changes.


### data_quality_statistics(data: Optional[Union[requests.DataQualityStatistics, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.DataQualityStatistics

Retrieve data quality statistics regarding the 'kelvin_data_quality_score' metric.

code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api import Client

client = Client()
result = client.client.api.data_quality.delete_bulk_data_quality()
